name: Apis Bull
shortDescription: The Apis Bull, in the context of Tarot, is an allegorical figure associated with the Wheel of Fortune and World cards, often representing the zodiac sign Taurus, stability, and the element earth.
description: The Apis Bull, within the Tarot, is not a literal creature but rather a symbolic representation drawn from ancient Egyptian mythology. In Egyptian lore, Apis was a sacred bull worshiped in the Memphis region, believed to be the living incarnation of the god Ptah and later associated with Osiris. In the Tarot, the Apis Bull typically appears on the Wheel of Fortune and the World card, positioned in one of the corners alongside the Lion, Eagle, and Man (or Angel). These four figures are often interpreted as representing the four fixed signs of the zodiac (Taurus, Leo, Scorpio, and Aquarius, respectively) and the four elements (Earth, Fire, Water, and Air). The Apis Bull symbolizes Taurus, the earth element, and the qualities of stability, groundedness, strength, and material wealth. It embodies the steadfast nature of the earth and the patient, determined approach to life often associated with Taurian traits. Its presence on the Wheel of Fortune suggests that even amidst the cycles of change and chance, there are enduring qualities and solid foundations to rely upon. On the World card, the Apis Bull signifies integration and mastery of the material world, representing the successful culmination of one's endeavors and the embodiment of earthly abundance.
appearance: Symbolic representation; often depicted as a winged bull or simply a bull's head.

sources.title: The Pictorial Key to the Tarot
sources.author: A.E. Waite
